results.alt: The "alarm jellyfish" — when attacked, it emits a brilliant rotating pinwheel of blue light to attract larger predators to its attacker. Atolla Jellyfish

Tiny photophores cover its body, each producing blue light. Every spring, millions rise to the surface of Toyama Bay in a dazzling display. Firefly Squid

Dense thickets of staghorn coral create a labyrinthine habitat. Their calcium carbonate branches reach toward the surface like antlers, providing shelter for thousands of species. Staghorn Forest Branching corals form underwater cathedrals

When darkness falls, the reef transforms. Moray eels slither from crevices, octopuses emerge to hunt, and coral polyps extend their tentacles to feed on passing plankton. Reef at Night Nocturnal hunters emerge under moonlight

A vibrant ecosystem where hard and soft corals compete for space and sunlight. Parrotfish graze on algae while clownfish dart through anemone tendrils. Shallow Reef Garden Sun-dappled corals in crystalline waters
